---
name: Correction or erratum
about: A specific claim in an entry is wrong, misleading, or has a citation problem. Smaller than a full update.
title: "[correction] "
labels: "correction"
---

## Entry affected

**Path:** <!-- e.g. `content/anti-analysis/01-anti-debug.md` -->

**Section:** <!-- e.g. "Mechanics → /proc/self/status probe" -->

## What's wrong

<!-- Quote the offending line(s) verbatim. -->

## What's correct

<!-- State the corrected claim precisely. -->

## Source

<!-- Cite the source for the correction: a man page, a documentation
page, an experiment you ran, a CVE, a paper. Uncited corrections are
hard to act on. -->

## Severity

<!-- factual-error (the claim is plainly wrong), misleading-but-true
(technically accurate but easy to misread), typo, broken-link,
citation-needed -->

## Suggested patch

<!-- Optional. A PR or a diff is even better than a description. -->
